topic: Perplexity AI search methodology, answer synthesis patterns

entries.a: Vane is a privacy-focused AI answering engine that runs entirely on your own hardware.

entries.q: What types of AI providers does Vane support?
entries.a: Vane supports local LLMs through Ollama and cloud providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Groq.

entries.q: What search modes are available in Vane?
entries.a: Vane offers three search modes: Speed Mode for quick answers, Balanced Mode for everyday searches, and Quality Mode for deep research.
Vane can be installed using Docker or without Docker. Using Docker is highly recommended as it simplifies the setup process.

entries.q: What is SearxNG and how is it used in Vane?
entries.a: SearxNG is a search engine that Vane uses to provide web search results. It can be used as a bundled instance with Vane or as a separate instance.

entries.q: Can I use Vane with my own SearxNG instance?
entries.a: Yes, you can use Vane with your own SearxNG instance by running the slim version of Vane and configuring the SearxNG API URL.
Vane offers a range of features including smart search modes, support for multiple AI providers, cited sources, and a user-friendly interface with widgets and smart suggestions.
Yes, Vane is open-source and free, thanks to the generous support of its sponsors and the open-source community.
